title: Introduction to Machine Learning
content: Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence that enables systems to learn from data. Supervised learning uses labeled examples, while unsupervised learning discovers hidden patterns.
author: Alice Chen

title: Understanding Vector Databases
content: Vector databases store high-dimensional embeddings for similarity search. They use algorithms like HNSW and IVF to find approximate nearest neighbors efficiently.
author: Bob Kumar

title: Building Search Engines with Lucene
content: Apache Lucene provides the foundation for full-text search. It uses inverted indexes for fast term lookup and BM25 for relevance scoring.
author: Alice Chen

title: LLM Agents and Memory Systems
content: Large language model agents need persistent memory to maintain context across sessions. Memory can be working, episodic, or semantic, each serving different retrieval needs.
author: Carol Zhang

title: Hybrid Search: Combining Text and Vectors
content: Hybrid search merges keyword-based BM25 with vector similarity using Reciprocal Rank Fusion. This approach captures both lexical matches and semantic meaning.
author: Bob Kumar

title: Kubernetes for Microservices
content: Kubernetes orchestrates containerized applications across clusters. It handles scaling, load balancing, and self-healing for microservice architectures.
author: Dave Patel

title: gRPC vs REST: When to Choose What
content: gRPC uses Protocol Buffers for binary serialization and HTTP/2 for transport. It outperforms REST for internal service communication with strong typing and streaming support.
author: Eve Wilson

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Explained
content: RAG combines information retrieval with language generation. Documents are indexed, relevant passages retrieved at query time, then fed as context to the LLM for grounded answers.
author: Carol Zhang

title: Time-Series Data in Search Engines
content: Search engines can handle time-series data using recency decay scoring. Recent documents get boosted scores that decay exponentially over time, keeping results fresh.
author: Alice Chen

title: Embedding Models Compared
content: Popular embedding models include OpenAI text-embedding-3, Cohere embed-v3, and open-source alternatives like BGE and E5. Choice depends on dimension, latency, and domain.
author: Bob Kumar
